provisions.title: Citation
provisions.content: These Regulations may be cited as the Muhimbili National Hospital (Renal Services) Regulations, 2017.

provisions.title: Interpretation
provisions.content: In these Regulations unless the context requires otherwise— “ Act ” means the Muhimbili National Hospital Act; “ appointment ” means a temporary appointment and includes an acting appointment; “ Authorization Committee ” means the Hospital Transplantation Authorization Committee established under regulation 10 ; “ donor ” means a person who provides a kidney willingly for transplantation; “ health care providers ” means health care providers of the Hospital ; “ Hospital ” means Muhimbili National Hospital established by the Act ; “ Minister ” means the Minister responsible for health; “ nephrologist ” means a specialist who specialized with nephrology ; “ nephrology ” means a subspecialty medicine which deals with kidney diseases; “ recipient ” means a patient with Chronic Kidney Disease (CKD) requiring transplantation of kidney from a donor ; “ Urology ” means a subspecialty in surgery which deals with urinary and male genital system. Part II – Administration, organization and type of service provided

provisions.title: Administration
provisions.content: (1) The nephrology services shall be provided through the Renal Unit which shall jointly be under the Departments of Internal Medicine and the Department of Paediatrics and Child Health. (2) The Renal Unit who shall be headed by a nephrologist from either the Department of Internal Medicine or Paediatrics and Child Health. (3) During renal transplant, surgical services shall be conducted by the Urology Department.

provisions.title: Services provided
provisions.content: (1) Renal services shall be provided to patients of all categories who require the services and shall cover both outpatient and inpatient services. (2) Outpatient services shall be provided to patients referred to the Hospital as well as for follow up patients. (3) There shall be outpatient clinics consists of the following— (a) transplant clinic for patients who have undergone kidney transplantation; (b) donor clinic for prospective donors; (c) an outpatient's clinic which covers both public and private adult and paediatric patients. (4) Inpatient services shall be provided in the following manner— (a) patients with renal conditions admitted in the medical and paediatrics wards shall receive services from health care providers in the Renal Unit; (b) patients admitted in other wards with other specialties shall receive renal services from the Renal Unit after consultation between the respective ward and the Renal Unit whether in writing or by telephone; (c) consultants nephrologists, urologists and any other specialists, residents, registrars and interns shall be providing renal services in the clinics whereas for each clinic there shall be at least one consultant nephrologist . (5) Renal transplant services shall be provided to patients with kidney failure through haemodialysis, peritoneal dialysis and renal transplantation based on the services to be provided only when proven that they may be beneficial to the patient as shall be recommended by the consultant nephrologist . (6) The renal transplant service provided under sub-regulations (5) shall be based on the service to be provided when proven that the service may be beneficial to the patient as shall be recommended by the consultant nephrologist . Part III – Kidney transplantation

provisions.title: Kidney transplantation
provisions.content: (1) Kidney transplantation services shall be conducted jointly under the Departments of Internal Medicine and Urology . (2) The Hospital shall regulate the removal, storage and transplantation of kidneys to avoid commercial transactions in human organs.

provisions.title: Authority for removal of a kidney
provisions.content: (1) Any living person of not less than eighteen years of age may voluntarily authorize the removal of his kidney during his lifetime as per relevant practices, for therapeutic purposes. (2) The authorization referred to in sub regulation shall be made in the manner prescribed in Form No. 1 specified in the Schedule to these Regulations.

provisions.title: Transplantation to near relatives only
provisions.content: (1) No kidney removed from the donor shall be transplanted on the recipient unless the donor is the near relative to the recipient . (2) For purposes of these Regulations, near relatives who may be allowed to donate shall include the following— (a) spouse; (b) child; (c) parent; (d) sibling; (e) grandparent; (f) grandchild; (g) cousin, nephew, niece; (h) uncle; or (i) aunt. (3) In certifying the relationship between the donor and recipient , the pathologist shall fill Form No. 4 specified in the Schedule to these Regulations.

provisions.title: Transplant coordinator
provisions.content: (1) There shall be a Transplant Coordinator who shall be appointed by the Executive Director of the Hospital . (2) The Transplant Coordinator shall have the qualifications of a nurse or social worker from amongst the staff of the Hospital . (3) The functions of the Transplant Coordinator shall be to— (a) receive and process all relevant documents related to prospective donors and recipients and ensure that documents are duly filled prior to submission to the Authorization Committee as per requirements of these Regulations; (b) compile all applications for kidney transplantation, file them each in its own file; (c) liaison with the Secretary, to convene, prepare and coordinate meetings of the Authorization Committee ; (d) coordinate medical meetings for renal transplant; (e) make sure that the proceedings of the Authorization Committee are video recorded and kept in safe custody; (f) ensure that outcomes of the decisions of the Authorization Committee are dully notified to the applicants; (g) ensure that all conditions of approval by the Authorization Committee are fulfilled.

provisions.title: Application for transplantation
provisions.content: (1) The donor and the recipient shall make a application for approval of the removal and transplantation of the kidney to the Authorization Committee in the manner prescribed in Form No. 2 specified in the Schedule these Regulations. (2) The Form referred to under sub regulation (1) shall be accompanied with other important documents as shall be prescribed therein. (3) The application and all documents shall be submitted to the Transplant Coordinator who shall process the same and forward them for submission to the Authorization Committee .

provisions.title: Medical meeting
provisions.content: (1) There shall be medical meetings which shall be held to deliberate on the prospective kidney recipient and donor reports. (2) The members of the medical meetings shall be as follows— (a) nephrologist ; (b) urologist (c) Transplant Coordinator; (d) social worker; and (e) donor and recipient . (3) The medical meetings shall be video recorded and the Transplant Coordinator shall ensure that the video recorded are kept in a safe custody.

provisions.title: Authorization committee
provisions.content: (1) There shall be an Authorization Committee which shall consists of not less than seven members appointed by the Board of Trustees of the Muhimbili National Hospital . (2) The members of the Authorization Committee shall be— (a) the Director of Medical Services who shall be the Chairman; (b) two members who are medical doctors but not involved in the renal transplant within the Hospital , appointed by the Executive Director; (c) a senior officer conversant with health matter appointed by the Ministry; (d) a senior social worker from within the Hospital ; (e) a senior lawyer of high integrity, social standing and credibility preferably a Retired High Court Judge; (f) a senior doctor from any one of the private hospitals within Dar es Salaam region; (g) two religious leaders, one representing Christian and the other representing Muslim. (3) The appointment of members under this regulation shall consider both technical and gender balance. (4) The Hospital ’s Head of Legal Unit or his representative shall be the Secretary to the Authorization Committee . (5) The medical practitioner who shall be part of the renal transplant team for carrying out transplantation operation shall not be a member of the Authorization Committee constituted by the Hospital . (6) Meetings of the Authorization Committee shall be convened upon receipt of at least three applications for kidney transplantation. (7) The quorum of the Authorization Committee shall be half of the members, two out of whom present shall be from within the Hospital .

provisions.title: Scrutiny of applications
provisions.content: (1) The Secretary shall circulate copies of the applications received from the proposed donors and recipients to all members of the Authorization Committee along with the supporting documents within two weeks after they are received in completion. (2) During the meeting, the Authorization Committee shall take note of all the relevant contents and documents and if any document or information is found to be inadequate or doubtful, explanation shall be sought from the applicants or any necessary verification may be sought through the authority concerned. (3) For purposes of these Regulations, the following documents shall be evaluated— (a) documentary evidence of the relationship including relevant birth certificates, marriage certificate, other relationship certificate or verification through letters from relevant authorities; (b) documentary evidence of identity and residence of the proposed donor , such as voter’s identity card, passport or driving license, family photographs depicting the donor and proposed recipient along with other near relatives; (c) if in the opinion of the Authorization Committee , the relationship is not conclusive by evaluation of the documentary evidence, the Authorization Committee may direct further medical tests, such as DNA profiling; (d) if the documentary evidence and medical tests does not establish a genetic relationship between the donor and the recipient , the same procedure shall be adopted on both or at least one parent, and if parents are not available, the same procedure shall be adopted on such relatives of donor and recipient as are available and willing to be tested, failure of which, genetic relationship between the donor and the recipient shall be deemed to have not been established; (e) where the proposed transplantation is between a spouses, the Authorization Committee shall evaluate the status and duration of the marriage and ensure that documents such as the marriage certificate, photographs and any other documents are kept for records along with the information on the number and age of children, family photograph showing the entire family, birth certificates showing the particulars of parents.

provisions.title: Approval for kidney transplantation
provisions.content: (1) The approval for kidney transplantation by the Authorization Committee shall be issued in writing. (2) The format of the approval of the Authorization Committee shall be as prescribed in Form No. 5 specified in the Schedule to these Regulations. (3) The Authorization Committee shall state in writing its reason for rejecting or approving the application in the Form No. 5 specified in the Schedule. (4) The approval form referred to under this regulation shall be signed by two members together with the Chairman.

provisions.title: Factors to be considered on Authorization
provisions.content: The approval of the Authorization Committee shall be made subject to the following factors — (a) the donor to be subjected to all such medical tests as required at the relevant stages to determine his or her biological capacity and compatibility to donate the kidney; (b) the physical and mental evaluation of the donor has to be done to establish whether the donor is in proper state of health and it has been certified by the registered medical practitioner that donor is— (i) not mentally challenged; and (ii) fit to donate the kidney, as specified in Form No.3 prescribed in the Schedule to these Regulations.

provisions.title: Decision by the authorization committee
provisions.content: (1) The Authorization Committee shall within twenty one days from the date of receipt of the application, make the decision. (2) The Authorization Committee shall expedite its decision making process and use its discretion prudently and practically in all such cases, where the patient requires transplantation on urgent basis.

provisions.title: Appeals
provisions.content: (1) Any person aggrieved by the decision of the Authorization Committee may, within fourteen days from the date of the decision, appeal to the Minister (2) The appeal under this regulation shall be in writing, accompanied with the decision of the Authorization Committee . (3) The Minister may, on considering the appeal referred to under sub regulation (1) , direct a re assessment of the application by the Authorization Committee if the Minister is satisfied that there are valid reasons to do so. Part V – Fees for renal services [Please note: Numbering as in original.]

provisions.title: Cost sharing in renal services
provisions.content: (1) A Patient receiving renal services at the Hospital shall contribute to the cost of his treatment by paying the amount of money which shall be determined by the management and approved by the Hospital ’s Board of Trustees from time to time. (2) The Hospital shall, through its Social Welfare Department scrutinize patients who fall under the various groups of exemptions from contributing to health services in accordance with the National Health Policy of 2007. (3) The Hospital may exempt patients of the categories of exemption from cost sharing in renal services. (4) The Ministry of Health shall provide subsidy to the Hospital in order to sustain renal services. Schedule Forms [Please note: Forms have not been reproduced.
